title: Potential Trips — Sept 2022 cheap-flights longlist
html:
Potential Trips — Sept 2022 cheap-flights longlist

Source: 2022-06-30 Google Doc — Yahir scraping Google Flights for late-August / early-September 2022 cheap roundtrips. Four anchor cities priced: Singapore $823, Jakarta $874, Cairo $854, Nairobi (Kenya) $912. Plus prose riffing on adding Vietnam (Hanoi or HCMC) or Marrakesh as side-quests.

TL;DR

  • Four flight quotes — Singapore $823 (Sep 2–15), Jakarta $874 (Aug 28–Sep 13), Cairo $854 (Aug 27–Sep 12), Nairobi $912 (Aug 28–Sep 12). All RT, all 2-week windows.
  • Vietnam as the affordability play — "Got o vietnam, Hanoi or Hiochiu Minh, then travel to either one of those during the 2 week trip. Probably most affordable" — pre-cursor to the actual Ha Giang / Vietnam loop he eventually did.
  • Cairo as the tourist-tax warning — "Go to Cairo, then travel to alexandria. Will be pricey witht he tourist shit" — prefigures the Egypt / White Desert arc.
  • Marrakesh as the curve-ball — "Go to Markannesh — Curve ball trip" — single-mention, never followed up in cataloged notes.
  • Companion to other scrappy travel longlists — Yahir's repeating habit of pricing 4–6 destinations and picking the cheapest viable one.

Pattern observed

The 2022 list reads as the dry-run for the 2024 pre Asia mental scaffolding — pricing windows, comparing tourist-tax overhead, identifying side-quest extensions from a primary hub. This ingest doesn't catalogue the actual September 2022 trip outcome (he was at OSU in fall 2022 — likely the trip never went), but the priced cities here (Vietnam, Egypt) are exactly the ones that materialize in the 2024 study abroad / Ha Giang sequence two years later. The longlist functions as an ambition log — what stuck, did get visited; what didn't (Singapore, Jakarta, Marrakesh, Nairobi) remains open.

Open questions

  • Did any of the September 2022 trips happen? (Doubtful — fall 2022 OSU coursework dump in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream is dense; no trip mentions.)
  • Why did Vietnam specifically stick (he did it in 2024) when the others didn't?
